metric_value: Athlon XP 2000+ (1.667GHz) rated equivalent to Intel P4 2.0GHz Willamette
notes: AMD named processors with GHz-equivalent model numbers rather than actual clock speed

metric_value: No Athlon XP 2000+ results filed with BAPCo as of Feb 15, 2002
notes: SYSmark 2001 comprised >1/3 of AMD's GHz-E score; BAPCo requires all licensees to file results

metric_value: ~33.3% (one-half Office Productivity plus two-thirds Content Creation)
notes: Aberdeen calculated the fraction of score attributable to SYSmark 2001

metric_value: Benchmark results updated after Arthur Andersen audit to include bug fix favoring AMD
notes: Bug fix found and incorporated after audit; improves AMD scores

metric_name: Intel SYSmark Scores vs AMD-Reported Intel Scores
metric_value: Intel's own Bapco results exceed AMD-reported Intel scores on same processor
notes: Lower Intel scores reported by AMD improve AMD's relative GHz-E rating

metric_value: Winbench 2000 uses DirectX 7.0; AMD/Intel systems tested under DirectX 8.1
notes: No real user would run older DirectX on Windows XP system

metric_value: AMD will be forced to abandon GHz-E and introduce new rating methodology within 2002
notes: Aberdeen prediction based on methodology decay rate

metric_value: Announced October 2001 as strategic alternative to GHz-E
notes: Aberdeen applauded TPI but noted it had stalled under GHz-E press releases

metric_value: P4 2.0A GHz Northwood (130nm, larger cache) outperforms 2.0GHz Willamette at same clock speed
notes: Northwood release made Athlon 2000+ GHz-E comparison immediately invalid

metric_value: AMD's 1/3-1/3-1/3 office/content/gaming workload mix not representative of many users
notes: Real-world workload mix is highly personal; no one-size-fits-all metric is feasible

metric_value: Business Winstone 2001 (I/O-intensive system benchmark) included to measure processor performance
notes: I/O benchmarks measure system vs. system (Dell vs. Compaq), not processor vs. processor

metric_value: European ads reported GHz-E model numbers as actual GHz; could constitute deceptive trade practices
notes: Aberdeen cited ads as 'deceptive at best and downright illegal at worst'

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends AMD examine SPECcpu 2000 before advancing TPI
notes: SPECcpu 2000 is not tied to specific applications but is industry-accepted

metric_value: GHz-E methodology projected to break down completely within 90 days of any measurement
notes: Software patches, new benchmarks, new Intel CPUs all erode equivalency quickly

metric_value: AA attestation covers only the 2000+ model; discrepancies between AA results and Bapco site unexplained
notes: Aberdeen notes no smoking gun but calls for AA to explain discrepancies

metric_value: AMD processors are efficient for many applications and do not need GHz-E to deserve market respect
notes: Aberdeen's balanced conclusion: critique of marketing, not the processors themselves
